lookup_component.description: Look up full metadata for a component type from the component database. Returns all properties, events, methods, and their types.

component_type.description: The component type name, e.g. Button, Label

lookup_screen.description: Look up the saved state of a screen including its component tree. Note: this reads from the server's last-saved data, which may not reflect unsaved changes. For the current screen, the component tree in the context messages above is the authoritative source. Use this tool only for non-current screens.

add_component.description: Add a new component to the current screen. Some components MUST be nested inside a specific parent: Ball/ImageSprite require a Canvas, ChartData2D/Trendline require a Chart, and Marker/LineString/Polygon/Rectangle/Circle/FeatureCollection require a Map. For these, you MUST set the parent_name parameter to the parent component's instance name.

component_name.description: Instance name — must start with a letter and contain only letters, digits, and underscores (no spaces, hyphens, or special characters). Must not be a Java/YAIL reserved word. Use descriptive PascalCase (e.g. SubmitButton, ScoreLabel), not incremental names like Button1

parent_name.description: Parent container instance name. Required for components with mandatory nesting (e.g. ChartData2D must specify its Chart parent). Defaults to screen root if omitted.

delete_component.description: Delete a component from the current screen.

set_property.description: Set a property value on a component. Color values must be in &HAARRGGBB form (e.g. &HFF0000FF for blue) — never &AARRGGBB, #AARRGGBB, 0x..., or signed decimals.

value.description: Property value (type depends on property). Colors: &HAARRGGBB. Booleans: "True"/"False". Numbers: string-encoded.

new_name.description: New component name — must start with a letter and contain only letters, digits, and underscores. Must not be a Java/YAIL reserved word

write_block.description: Create or replace a top-level block (event handler, global variable, or procedure) using YAIL code. The YAIL form head identifies the block type and target. If a block with the same identity already exists, it is replaced (upsert semantics) — do NOT pair this with a delete_block for the same identity. Each block identity may be mutated at most once per batch; the server rejects conflicting write_block/delete_block calls that target the same identity.

yail.description: Complete YAIL S-expression for the top-level block. Must be one of: (define-event ComponentName EventName ...), (define-generic-event ComponentType EventName ...), (def g$varName initialValue), (def (p$procName $param1 ...) body) for procedures without return, or (def-return (p$procName $param1 ...) body) for procedures with return

delete_block.description: Delete a top-level block (event handler, global variable, or procedure). The identifier format matches the YAIL form head tokens. Each block identity may be mutated at most once per batch; the server rejects a delete_block whose identity is already targeted by another delete_block or a write_block in the same response.

block.description: Block identifier using YAIL head tokens, e.g. 'define-event Button1 Click', 'define-generic-event Button Click', 'def g$score', 'def p$factorial', 'def-return p$myFunc'

toggle_editor.description: Switch the editor view between Designer and Blocks. This tool MUST be called ALONE — do not combine it with any other tools in the same response. After the toggle is confirmed, continue with the operations that require the new view.

view.description: The editor view to switch to

switch_screen.description: Switch the active screen context. This tool MUST be called ALONE — do not combine it with any other tools in the same response. After the switch is confirmed, continue with the operations that require the new screen.

create_screen.description: Create a new screen in the project. The screen name must start with a letter and contain only letters, digits, and underscores (no spaces, hyphens, or special characters). Must not be a Java/YAIL reserved word. Use descriptive PascalCase (e.g. LoginScreen, Settings), not incremental names like Screen2.

delete_screen.description: Delete a screen from the project.

set_project_property.description: Set a project-level property (theme, colors, sizing, etc.). Color values must be in &HAARRGGBB form (e.g. &HFF0000FF for blue) — never &AARRGGBB, #AARRGGBB, 0x..., or signed decimals.

propose_plan.description: Propose an execution plan for the user to review before making changes. Each step targets a specific screen and describes what will be done. Steps targeting different screens can execute in parallel. Use lookup_component and lookup_screen first to research the project, then propose a complete plan.

summary.description: Brief overall description of what the plan accomplishes

screen.description: Target screen name. Use __project__ for project-level operations (create screen, project properties)

depends_on.description: Step IDs that must complete before this step starts
